user.title: Profile Setup
user.description: Create a new agent profile with a unique name and personality.

data_description.profile_name: A friendly name for this conversation profile.
data_description.server_type: Choose which provider or inference server this profile uses.

server.title: Server Configuration
server.description: Configure your inference server URL and MCP server port.

data_description.lmstudio_url: The base URL for the local or OpenAI-compatible server used by this profile.
data_description.api_key: The API key or bearer token required by this provider.
data_description.mcp_port: The local port used by the shared MCP server.

data_description.model_name: The LLM model this profile should use.
data_description.system_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective system prompt. Edit it to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.
data_description.technical_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective technical instructions. Edit them to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.

model_fields.name: Model
model_fields.description: Choose the model this profile should use.

data_description.model_name: The LLM model this profile should use.

prompts.name: Prompts
prompts.description: Review the current effective prompts, copy them, or edit them for this profile.

data_description.system_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective system prompt. Edit it to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.
data_description.technical_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective technical instructions. Edit them to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.

conversation.name: Conversation
conversation.description: Behavior, follow-up handling, and voice-output settings for this profile.

data_description.control_home_assistant: Allow this profile to execute Home Assistant actions instead of only answering questions.
data_description.response_mode: Controls whether the assistant ends immediately or asks natural follow-up questions when appropriate.
data_description.follow_up_phrases: Comma-separated phrases used to detect follow-up style responses.
data_description.end_words: Comma-separated words or phrases that should end the conversation.
data_description.clean_responses: Trim unnecessary formatting or filler to keep spoken responses cleaner.

performance.name: Performance
performance.description: Model-response limits, execution limits, timeout, and debugging.

data_description.temperature: Controls response randomness. Lower values are more deterministic.
data_description.max_tokens: Caps how many tokens the model may generate in one response.
data_description.max_history: How many prior conversation messages to keep in context.
data_description.max_iterations: Caps how many tool-calling turns the assistant may use for one request.
data_description.timeout: How long to wait for a provider response before timing out.
data_description.debug_mode: Enable more detailed logging for troubleshooting.

provider.name: Provider
provider.description: Provider-specific settings shown only when they apply.

data_description.ollama_keep_alive: How long Ollama should keep the selected model loaded in memory.
data_description.ollama_num_ctx: Override the context window size passed to Ollama. Use 0 to keep the model default.

data_description.enabled_tools: Optional. Leave blank to inherit whatever tool families are enabled on the shared MCP server. If you choose specific items here, this profile will be narrowed to that subset only. Each selected family must also be enabled in the shared MCP server settings to actually work. Device tools add physical-device discovery. Response service tools add native HA reads like forecasts and calendars. Recorder tools answer history questions. Assist bridge tools expose HA's native Assist API. Calculator tools add math and unit conversion. Music Assistant tools add richer music search and playback.

mcp_server.title: Shared MCP Server Settings
mcp_server.description: ⚠️ These settings affect ALL profiles. Changes here will impact every MCP Assist conversation agent.

data_description.mcp_port: The local port used by the shared MCP server.
data_description.allowed_ips: Optional comma-separated IP addresses or CIDR ranges that may connect in addition to localhost.

search.name: Search
search.description: Configure optional web search tools exposed by the shared MCP server.

data_description.search_provider: Choose whether the shared MCP server should expose web search tools, and which provider to use.
data_description.brave_api_key: Required only when Brave Search is selected.

discovery.name: Discovery
discovery.description: Control how the shared MCP server builds and returns discovery results.

data_description.enable_gap_filling: Builds a compact system index to keep discovery fast and context-aware.
data_description.max_entities_per_discovery: Caps how many entities a single discovery call may return.

tools.name: Tools
tools.description: Configure optional tool capabilities exposed by the shared MCP server, including web search.

data_description.search_provider: Choose whether the shared MCP server should expose web search tools, and which provider to use.
data_description.brave_api_key: Required only when Brave Search is selected.
data_description.enable_weather_forecast_tool: Expose the dedicated Home Assistant weather forecast tool. When disabled, weather forecast questions will not use the weather MCP endpoint or weather response services.
data_description.enabled_tools: Choose which optional tool families the shared MCP server exposes to profiles and external MCP clients. Device tools add physical-device discovery. Response service tools add native HA service-response reads. Recorder tools add history analysis. Assist bridge tools expose HA's native Assist API. Calculator tools add math and unit conversion. Music Assistant tools add rich music search and playback.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the inference server. Make sure it's running and accessible.
error.no_models: No models loaded on the server. Please load a model first.
error.invalid_model: The specified model is not available on the server.
error.invalid_ip: Invalid IP address or CIDR range format. Use comma-separated values like: 172.30.0.0/16, 192.168.1.100
error.invalid_json: Invalid JSON format. Please check your MCP servers configuration.
error.invalid_api_key: Invalid API key. Please check and try again.
error.auth_failed: Authentication failed. Please verify your API key.

abort.already_configured: A profile with this name already exists. Please choose a different name.

data_description.technical_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective technical instructions. Edit them to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.

advanced_settings.name: Advanced
advanced_settings.description: Expert tuning for model behavior, limits, provider-specific options, timeout, and debugging.

data_description.max_tokens: Caps how many tokens the model may generate in one response.
data_description.max_history: How many prior conversation messages to keep in context.

data_description.timeout: How long to wait for a provider response before timing out.
data_description.debug_mode: Enable more detailed logging for troubleshooting.
data_description.ollama_keep_alive: How long Ollama should keep the selected model loaded in memory.
data_description.ollama_num_ctx: Override the context window size passed to Ollama. Use 0 to keep the model default.

data_description.enabled_tools: Optional. Leave blank to inherit whatever tool families are enabled on the shared MCP server. If you choose specific items here, this profile will be narrowed to that subset only. Each selected family must also be enabled in the shared MCP server settings to actually work. Device tools add physical-device discovery. Response service tools add native HA reads like forecasts and calendars. Recorder tools answer history questions. Assist bridge tools expose HA's native Assist API. Calculator tools add math and unit conversion. Music Assistant tools add richer music search and playback.
